GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Finding the next big thing in fashion (and Vogue is on the judging panel)", "Fashion is as synonymous to the Melbourne Cup Carnival as a sweep, but that's not the only way to back a winner this spring. Myer Fashions on the Field also includes the highly coveted Emerging Designer Award, now in its third year. Intended to uncover a fresh crop of racing fashion talent and provide exposure on a global scale to the winner, 2020 sees the title get a new name in line with changes brought on by the pandemic: the Myer Fashions on your Front Lawn Emerging Designer Award. Winning designs are considered on a range of different criteria. Creativity and innovation, of course, is paramount. A panel of esteemed judges, including Network 10's Melissa Leong and a Vogue editor, will rank designs on imagination, as well as vision and the articulation of the design process. In addition, the designs are rated on all of the skills required to manufacture — technique, craft and attention to detail. Progressive designers with a new point of view have fared well in previous years, with winners including West Australian-based racing season favourite Kate Watts from Only One Ashley and Queensland haute couturier, Jason Chetcuti of Chetcuti Couture, who studied the craft in Paris and spent time at London label Ralph and Russo. This year, the panel of industry experts select their favourites to make up a prestigious top 10. These finalists will then be asked to present their design digitally. The winner of the 2020 Myer Fashions on your Front Lawn Emerging Designer Award receives a prize worth more than $25 000. Aside from the accolades, the stellar first place package includes a full page in the February 2021 issue of Vogue Australia, as well as a page in the Victoria Racing Club's Inside Headquarters magazine. There is also a Myer gift card, Antler luggage and a TV package from TCL, plus a four-night stay at Sofitel Melbourne on Collins. Not to be deterred by current affairs, the annual celebration of dressing up for the races continues вЂ“ just a little differently this year. The beloved fashion catwalk competition for the women's and men's categories goes online for the very first time in its storied history, opening up the competition Nationally starting September 12 and running until mid-October. The Myer Fashions on your Front Lawn Millinery Award also runs at the same time, with both emerging and established designers encouraged to enter before the cut off on October 13. Find out more about each of these High Fashion categories at fashionsonyourfrontlawn.com.au. Applications for the Myer Fashions on your Front Lawn Emerging Designer Award are open now, and close at 5pm on Tuesday 13th October. So, time to get off and racing."
